title: arthraxon hispidus f. japonicus
intro: Arthraxon hispidus f. japonicus, also known as Japanese joint grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and treating various respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Arthraxon hispidus f. japonicus are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or powders for use in traditional medicine.
